objective: Configure the GitHub remote MCP server
prompt: Which URL configures the remote GitHub MCP server?

options.text: https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/
https://github.com/mcp/
https://api.github.com/copilot/mcp/
https://mcp.github.dev/

explanation: GitHub documents the remote GitHub MCP endpoint as https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/. Authentication can use the supported OAuth flow or a suitably scoped token.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Setting up the GitHub MCP Server

prompt: An enterprise wants developers to use only MCP servers listed in its approved registry. Which policy should it select?

options.text: Allow all
Registry only
Read-only repositories
Required reviewers

explanation: The Registry only policy restricts MCP use to servers represented in the configured registry. The registry URL and access policy are managed separately from repository review controls.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Configure MCP server access

prompt: Before running an agent in CI, which two environment checks are most important? Select two.

options.text: The working directory, repository ref, and available dependencies
The secrets, network destinations, and permissions available to the job
The color theme used by the runner
Whether the model can rename the default branch

explanation: An agent's execution context includes the checked-out code and installed tools as well as credentials, network access, and job permissions. Those constraints determine what the agent can safely and successfully do.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 environment interaction objectives

prompt: How should a coding agent normally deliver repository changes?

options.text: Commit directly to the protected default branch
Work on an isolated branch and open a pull request for review
Upload changed files to an untracked external location
Disable branch rules until the agent finishes

explanation: Branch isolation contains the work, and a pull request provides the standard review, validation, and audit path before changes reach the default branch.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Get started with Copilot agents on GitHub

prompt: Which failure is the best candidate for an automatic retry?

options.text: A transient HTTP 503 from a dependency
A policy rule that explicitly denies the action
A deterministic test failure caused by incorrect code
A missing required approval

explanation: Transient service failures may succeed after bounded retry with backoff. Policy denials, deterministic defects, and approval gates require correction or escalation rather than repeated execution.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 safe execution objectives

prompt: Before rewinding a Copilot CLI workspace to an earlier snapshot, which two effects should you account for? Select two.

options.text: Manual edits made after the snapshot can also be reverted
New files created after the snapshot can be removed
Only the most recent Copilot-generated line is reverted
Every later session message is automatically preserved

explanation: A Git-based workspace rewind can restore the entire snapshot state, including reverting later manual changes and removing files created afterward. It is broader than undoing one generated edit.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Cancel and roll back Copilot CLI changes

prompt: An agent has exhausted its bounded retries and still receives an authorization error. What should happen next?

options.text: Retry indefinitely
Escalate with the error, attempted actions, and relevant logs
Replace the error with a successful status
Grant an organization-owner token automatically

explanation: After the retry policy is exhausted, the workflow should stop and provide enough evidence for a human or owning system to diagnose the access problem. Automatically broadening privileges would violate least privilege.

source.label: Microsoft Learn — GH-600 safe execution objectives

prompt: Which two records provide useful accountability for an agent tool call? Select two.

options.text: The tool name, sanitized inputs, result status, and timestamp
The workflow run, commit, or pull request associated with the action
The full secret value used to authenticate
The agent's private hidden reasoning

explanation: Traceability links an observable tool action to its execution context and outcome. Secrets must be redacted, and private reasoning is neither necessary nor appropriate audit evidence.

source.label: GitHub Docs — Risks and mitigations for Copilot cloud agent
